Child and Adolescent Health Program and Policy
VISION
Every child, youth, adolescent, and young adult has 
						access to a healthy life.
MISSION
To promote the health and well being of children, youth, 
						adolescents, and young adults in Los Angeles County 
						through collaboration with other public health programs, 
						private agencies and community-based organizations.
The Child and Adolescent Health Program and Policy 
						(CAHPP) works to identify community organizations, 
						businesses, and other health partners to create a new 
						adolescent health collaborative in Los Angeles County to 
						address health disparities which impact youth and their 
						families. In February 2004, the Los Angeles 
						County-Department of Health Services, Child Abuse 
						Prevention Program merged with the Child and Adolescent 
						Health unit. CAHPP continues to provide child abuse 
						prevention services and resources to providers and 
						families within Los Angeles County.
PRIORITIES
(click for more information)
Adolescent Health
Asthma
Breastfeeding 
						Promotion
Childhood Obesity Prevention
For more information, call the Los Angeles County Child 
						and Adolescent Health Program Policy @ (213) 639-6400.
